programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Hunting Ground

programme.desc: Two of Jessica and Shanell's siblings are being lured back to The Order by their father, Daniel.

programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: The Prophet's Son
programme.desc: Jessica meets with Raymond Jeffs, son of Warren Jeffs, the notorious leader and prophet of the FLDS polygamist cult. Raymond pleads for their help getting his only brother out of the group.

programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: Forbidden Love
programme.desc: Jessica and Amanda receive a vague message from Megan, a young girl who recently escaped the FLDS polygamist cult, asking for help getting her boyfriend, Chris, out.

programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: Family Under Fire
programme.desc: Jessica and Andrea receive word from Jesse, a father desperate for his family to escape the polygamous group known as the AUB.

programme.title: Intervention: Family of Addicts: Tom and John
programme.desc: John and Tom are brothers who spent their formative years working with their parents and grandparents in a successful business. While they appeared to be a close knit, hard-working family, a dark family history of drug abuse entrapped the brothers.

programme.title: Intervention: Jason
programme.desc: Jason was born to a strict Southern Baptist family, and when his mother revealed she was a lesbian they were banished from the church. Left with no supervision, Jason turned to drugs and alcohol.

programme.title: Intervention: Leslie
programme.desc: Leslie is a Sunday school teacher, PTA member, soccer mom of three and a raging alcoholic. After countless arrests and DUIs her husband is hoping to find help for the mother of his children before it's too late.

programme.title: Intervention: Diana
programme.desc: Diana exchanged her maternal apron for an alcohol and meth addiction, which is ruining her marriage, family and sense of self-respect.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Fraudway
programme.desc: Q, Joe, Sal and Murr one-up each other while trying not to laugh, and team up for a lunch that’s for the birds. Later, tonight’s big loser hits the Broadway stage, where he’s treated to a classic New York welcome by an agitated crowd.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Paternity Test
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr pose as concierges in a hotel lobby, and then commit insurance fraud with the help of some confused strangers. Afterwards, tonight's loser suffers a personal punishment that's anything but child's play.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Take Me Out at the Ballgame
programme.desc: The guys get drenched at a water park, and tonight's loser strikes out while jockeying for a ball from Mets pitcher Noah Syndergaard.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Full Mental Jacket
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Q and Sal send blood pressure rates sky-high while posing as nurses, then stroll through the store with their wives. Plus, the big loser wishes he could check out early at a pop-up fashion show.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Show Stopper
programme.desc: Sal, Q, Joe and Murr are at Universal Studios in Hollywood. They start off serving up ridiculous surveys, then make things awkward while posing as maintenance workers. Plus, tonight’s big loser can't catch a break…no matter how many times he tries.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Sucks for You
programme.desc: Sal, Q, Joe and Murr bus their way through Katz’s Deli, then express some unpopular opinions at the park. Plus, tonight’s big loser must talk his way through some real highs and lows, in a punishment that truly sucks.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Well...
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr play a game of tag at a coffee shop, then convince shoppers to deliver sketchy messages at the grocery store. Plus, tonight's big loser might lose his mind in Impractical Jokers' first-ever non-punishment punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Prize Fighter
programme.desc: The guys get their checks bounced by customers who notice unusual charges, then help out coworkers with some peculiar trust exercises. Later, the big loser finds himself deep at a carnival game that's rigged for him to lose.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Sun-Fan Lotion
programme.desc: Murr, Q and Sal are at the massage parlor for much kneaded relaxation, and then ask strangers their opinions on questionable yearbook ads. Later, tonight's big loser has an awkward but fan-tastic time in the sun.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Urine Trouble
programme.desc: The guys play receptionists looking for suspicious names. The guys seek marriage advice from strangers after a private eye shares incriminating photos. The big loser goes from pissed off to pissed on in the matter of one punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Irritable Vowel Syndrome
programme.desc: Murr, Sal and Joe seek help from customers when escaping security at the grocery store, then tell bizarre tales to diners at a food court. Plus, the big loser is in consonant hell when he's left looking for a missing letter.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Bad Carma
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q showcase questionable products to participants, and then rescue strangers who don't need to be rescued. Later, the big loser must hit and run his mouth to get himself out of trouble.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Fast Feud
programme.desc: Four friends compete to embarrass each other in the ultimate hidden camera showdown.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Toll Booth Corrector
programme.desc: The guys pose as suspicious nutritionists trying to recruit clients, before quitting their jobs for strange reasons. Afterwards, the gang goes head-to-head in a wild debate judged by a stranger.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Sal, Joe, Murr and Q take a spin in a motorized chair hoping the kindness of strangers will keep them on track. Tonight's big loser walks away bruised and battered when he's forced to clear some tables in an unusual way.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Needy and the Greedy
programme.desc: The guys have fast fingers at a focus group and leave no cereal box unturned at the grocery store. Then, tonight's big loser opens a can of something fierce at a local donation center.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Card Against Humanity
programme.desc: Q, Murr, Joe and Sal have customers seeing red while working at an optician's office, then get too close for comfort eavesdropping on strangers' conversations. Plus, the big loser gives the business at a networking event.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Lady and the Tramp
programme.desc: The guys us a two-way mirror to get each other to crack, and tonight's loser gets taken for a ride that ends at the Statue of Liberty.

programme.title: Lost Resort: Unmasked Meltdowns
programme.desc: When Robin and Christine's fighting annoys the rest of the group, Meco and Claudia give them a piece of their mind. An unlikely friendship forms when the group gets vulnerable. A romance among the guests gets steamy.

programme.title: Lost Resort: Turn Up the Heat
programme.desc: Channelling ancestors turns out to be conflicting for guests and healer Ataseia. The Sexual Energy Healer threatens a burgeoning romance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Fe-mail
programme.desc: It's spa day for the Jokers, although for them it's anything but relaxing. Then, at the park, Sal reveals his secret identity while discussing the birds and bees of dolphins. Plus, the big loser finds himself a little overdressed.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Lost Boy
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Sal and Q network their way into some very uncomfortable business conversations, and go head to head in a photography presentation that's anything but picture perfect.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Up Loser's Creek
programme.desc: The jokers make some tough calls at a telemarketing agency that leaves everyone wondering if they're just phoning it in. Then, the guys' intentions get wishy-washy at a fountain in the mall. Plus, Q and Sal tap into their spiritual side down at the shore.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: In Poor Taste Buds
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr take to the court to serve up some seriously personal tennis lessons and then head to the park for a bewildering game of Now! The loser must put his face through the ultimate test during a food presentation.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 12
programme.desc: A man at a gas pump doesn't notice his car rolling away right behind him, while a bride and groom get sent to jail for DUI.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 13

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 14
programme.desc: Marvel at the man who drives his car through the front door and down the hallways of city hall. Another drunk driver is too intoxicated to order takeout, and a guy takes an expensive sports car for a test drive...and totals it.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Dumbest Drivers 16
programme.desc: An over sized man tries to outrun a cop car, while a forklift operator totals an entire warehouse full of alcohol! Plus, dumb drivers crash into the same unlucky bakery.

programme.title: The Misery Index: UK vs US Special
programme.desc: After some heated UK vs US banter, Jameela and the Tenderloins find out once and for all which of these great nations is truly the most miserable. Plus, Jameela has a big surprise fail for the Tenderloins.

programme.title: The Misery Index: When We Smash

programme.desc: Find out how many laser nipples one man can stand and who the sexy fireman who 'rescues' Sal from the bathroom of horrors really is.

programme.title: Botched: Human Dolls
programme.desc: Plastic surgeons Terry Dubrow and Paul Nassif treat a woman with a uni-boob, an actress who has had six surgeries in search of the perfect nose, and make a house call for Justin, a self-proclaimed human doll.

programme.title: Botched: Vagina Bomb!
programme.desc: Transgender star Kimber James asks the doctors to fix her botched breasts, nose and vagina. Paul tackles a tragic deviated septum and internet celebrity Renee Talley wants her famous botched bottom fixed.

programme.title: Botched: Like a Surgeon
programme.desc: A reality star seeks help to fix her botched boobs and stomach after her former plastic surgeon is allegedly killed by an unhappy patient. Paul fixes a pig-ish' nose and Terry struts his stuff with a celebrity impersonator.

programme.title: Botched: Most Outrageous Patients
programme.desc: Take a look at the top 10 most outrageous patients.

programme.title: Botched: Man Boobs
programme.desc: Dr. Dubrow helps a mother who looks like she's been pregnant for 12 years, Dr. Nassif takes on a woman who hasn't been able to close her mouth since college and the doctors meet with a gambler with a serious case of man boobs.

programme.title: Botched: Short Changed at the Nipple Bank
programme.desc: A transgender woman who got her rhinoplasty in Mexico seeks Dr. Nassif's help to fix her collapsed nose. Then, Dr. Dubrow tackles a botched double mastectomy, and a young woman travels from the UK to look faker than fake.

programme.title: Botched: Blowflish Lips and Crab Claw Bits
programme.desc: Dr. Nassif helps a woman with blowfish lips, while Dr. Dubrow takes on a difficult case of crab claw breasts. Later, a male model travels all the way from Prauge in pursuit of perfection.

programme.title: Botched: Seeing Double
programme.desc: A fitness enthusiast and nasal spray addict wants Paul to fix her nose, and a woman with deflated boobs comes to Terry for help. Later, the doctors meet the world's most identical twins.

programme.title: Botched: Plastic Fantastic
programme.desc: After almost dying during a procedure in Ecuador, an ex-Playboy model wants a second chance to fix her neck and nose. Terry tries to help a nurse with one big boob instead of two, and reality star Angelique Morgan wants to be even more plastic.

programme.title: Botched: Pinched Perfect
programme.desc: Paul helps a singer find his voice again with a revision rhinoplasty, and Terry reconstructs the chest of a young mother whose surgeon put in two different sized implants. Later, the doctors assist a woman with stretched earlobes.

programme.title: The Daily Pop
programme.desc: Your go-to for behind-the-scenes scoop, celebrity guests and entertainment conversation.

programme.title: Botched: Playground Trauma and a Pint-Sized Mama
programme.desc: A pint-sized Irish model travels across the pond for larger breasts while the doctors help a woman who almost died from a nightmarish tummy tuck. A young father with a busted nose hopes the docs can give him a nose just like his daughter's.

programme.title: Botched: 2,000CCs and Counting
programme.desc: A frightening surgical mishap in Colombia leaves a woman with franken-tits and the doctors salvage a man's dog bitten nose. While an Australian woman has a severe case of boob greed.

programme.title: Botched: Nothing Butt Trouble
programme.desc: A young woman visits the doctors and multiple silicone removal surgeries have left her with a distorted derriere, while a leggy painter needs her nose fixed to help lift her mother's guilty conscience.

programme.title: Botched: Muscles, Tucks and Forehead Flaps
programme.desc: Dr. Nassif takes on a patient who tried to treat her own skin cancer and ended up with a hole in her nose while Dr. Dubrow tackles a body builder's deflated breast.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Summer Grillin' Games, Part 2
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's summer grilling tournament continues with only seven GGG winners remaining. It's all about the boardwalk as the chefs make summer seafood dishes and spicy seaside sandwiches.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Summer Grillin' Games Part 3
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's Summer Grillin' Games continue, this time with carnival-themed games and dishes. Guy gives the chefs a funnel cake-sized twist by putting them in pairs to make a fried sweet-and-savory duo and a cheesy carnival classic.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Summer Grillin' Games Part 4
programme.desc: Guy Fieri treats the remaining five chefs to a Flavortown vacation, and one chef's destination dinner has a chance to earn 'Best Dish' an instant $5,000 and a spot in the finale. Then, the bottom four chefs compete to see whose trip will get cut short.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Summer Grillin' Games Finale
programme.desc: For the four chefs in Guy Fieri's summer bash finale, a beach ball determines which aisles the chefs get to shop for their summertime guilty pleasure. Then, they find feature ingredients for their ultimate grilled feast in an epic scavenger hunt.

programme.title: Chopped: Beat Bobby Flay: Battle 1
programme.desc: For this tournament event, 16 champions are back to defeat Chef Bobby Flay. In the premiere battle, a strangely sweet sandwich and beautiful seafood await the champs. Round two involves an unusual beef jerky product and a twist on hummus.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Going Global
programme.desc: Chef Richard Blais recruits TV personality Laura Vitale to try to knock Bobby Flay down a peg or two. They enlist the help of global food enthusiasts Chef Reza Setayesh and Chef Andy Gaynor to make Bobby's world spin.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Have Merci!
programme.desc: All bets are off in this fantastic showdown between French caterer Gui Alinat and his Dallas counterpart Kevin Ashade. Both of them will first be judged by Josh Capon and Alex Guarnaschelli before moving on to the next competition.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Do or Die
programme.desc: It's all on the line when Tex-Mex master Gregory Webb faces off against New Jersey's Peter Morris in this battle for supremacy. Tough New York City chefs Scott Conant and Geoffrey Zakarian have the call on who's got the skills to beat Bobby Flay.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: All in the Family
programme.desc: Sassy Miami chef Mika Leon brings the heat against Atlanta chef Michael Bertozzi. Chef Carla Hall and actor Jesse Tyler Ferguson decide who has what it takes to join their modern family for a food fight against Bobby Flay.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: In the Crosshairs
programme.desc: Game wardens enforce the law across Texas. A hunting club has a trespassing issue. People are caught attempting to illegally cross the Rio Grande. An argument between neighbors is mediated. Fishermen receive citations on the Gulf of Mexico.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Lying Dogs
programme.desc: The Game Wardens patrol the beach, lake and land, as summer is in full swing. Wardens educate a dog owner about leaving pets in the sun. An under-age drinker tells too many lies. Wardens inspect novice jet skiers on a busy recreational lake.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Stag Poaching
programme.desc: Texas Game Warden Gould responds to the call about an exotic red stag that was shot. In Hunt County, a plethora of warrants are dispersed for unpaid fishing violations, while Warden Flores and Williams join forces on the last day on patrol.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Family Lies
programme.desc: It's the start of hunting season, dubbed the 'Holy Day for Game Wardens.' Wardens Inman and Eddins investigate the illegal shooting of two deer. Then, Wardens Stapleton and McGee swap out bass fish in the Bois d'Arc Lake.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Thrill of the Hunt
programme.desc: A tense confrontation ensues when Wardens Scott and Gould respond to a call about an illegal deer hunting case. A pair of raccoons find their way to a gas station and must be trapped, and Warden Boone handles a disgruntled hunter.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: In Hot Water
programme.desc: Dockery responds to a call about an alligator found near a residential area. The game wardens find blood in a hunter's car after a hunter claimed he didn't shoot anything. Boone follows up on a call about five racoons making their way into someone's home.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Shrimp and Run
programme.desc: For Texas game wardens, the unexpected is all part of the routine. A shrimp boat tries to make a run for it. One warden makes some furry friends. A simple trespass warning turns into a trip to jail. A warden helps a woman spiraling out of control.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Thrill of the Hunt
programme.desc: A tense confrontation ensues when Wardens Scott and Gould respond to a call about an illegal deer hunting case. A pair of raccoons find their way to a gas station and must be trapped, and Warden Boone handles a disgruntled hunter.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Panhandle Poachers
programme.desc: Warden Marshall leads the charge on a poaching investigation. Warden Richards visits an allegedly illegal snake breeder. Wardens Gould and Hickerson try to save an injured pelican, while Warden Miller brings a caracara bird to rehab.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: In the Crosshairs
programme.desc: Game wardens enforce the law across Texas. A hunting club has a trespassing issue. People are caught attempting to illegally cross the Rio Grande. An argument between neighbors is mediated. Fishermen receive citations on the Gulf of Mexico.

programme.desc: Texas Game Warden Gould responds to the call about an exotic red stag that was shot. In Hunt County, a plethora of warrants are dispersed for unpaid fishing violations, while Warden Flores and Williams join forces on the last day on patrol.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Never Too Late
programme.desc: Lizzy tries to track down the owner of a dog tied up outside the rescue. An older couple comes looking for their first dog, but their choice requires some special temperament testing. And, when a dog develops allergies, Mariah looks for a natural cure.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Raised in Rescue
programme.desc: Return adopters visit Villalobos to find a dog that will feel at home on their boat. Tania and Mariah head out to rescue a stray that is hiding beneath a shipping container. And parolee Jammall starts to slip up, risking his future at the rescue.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: A Dream Realized
programme.desc: Two strays lead Lizzy and Villalobos employees on a wild goose chase. A grandmother comes to Villalobos looking for another canine companion, but her dog Daisy has other plans. And, Tania pursues her dream to write a children's book.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: The Villalobos Bowl
programme.desc: Before heading to New York City's Dog Bowl, Mariah and M2 prepare their most eligible rescues for the big day. Toney and Earl discover an emancipated stray dog roaming a nearby neighbourhood. A lone trucker comes to VRC to find a forever friend.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: You Are Free
programme.desc: Tania struggles to rescue a puppy that is hiding underneath a shed. A potential adopter comes to Villalobos hoping to find a companion for her dog, and she's only interested in seeing hounds. A beloved employee learns that he's finally off parole.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Pack of Puppies
programme.desc: A Harley-loving adopter comes to Villalobos from Michigan. Mariah and Lizzy struggle to catch a stray pit in New Orleans East. When a litter of black pit-mix puppies are dropped off at the Sanctuary, Tia and M2 turn to social media to adopt them out.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Escaping Death
programme.desc: When an aggressive dog is in danger of being put down, Tia heads to South Carolina to save him. A couple from Florida comes to VRC hoping to find a dog that will enjoy retired life with them. Parolee Toney Converse takes on more responsibility.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Saved from the Fight
programme.desc: Tia is called in to help rescue five injured dogs from a dog fighting situation. Parolee Spencer gets out of jail and returns to Villalobos. Tia asks Spencer to help out with an adoption to keep him on the straight and narrow.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Looking for a Home: Unexpected Development
programme.desc: Tia highlights a favourite pup still looking for a forever home. As Moe and Lizzy reveal they are expecting baby two, parolee Spencer announces he's expecting his first. And things don't go as planned when Lizzy's favorite canine goes up for adoption.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Twist of Fate
programme.desc: Tia and M2 take to the water to prepare a pit bull for her forever home. A new parolee comes to Villalobos from Boston, hoping stay on track so that he can make his teenage son proud. A stray dog is near death when Lizzy and Heidi find him.

programme.title: The Last Alaskans: Two Kills
programme.desc: Needing a big kill to sustain them through the winter, Heimo takes Krin and Scott to his best caribou hunting grounds. Tyler and Ashley want running water in their cabin.

programme.title: Movie: Barbershop 2: Back in Business

programme.desc: Things are going well for Calvin, Eddie and the rest of the crew at Calvin's Barbershop, but their neighbourhood is taking a turn for the worse. With gang members roaming the streets, the crew needs to work together to save the shop and the community.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Teacher's Pet
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars set their aim on a World War II era bomb-sight. Corey and the Old Man check out a stock ticker from the 1930s. Antwaun shows interest in learning the pawn business, and Chumlee agrees to teach him.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Bossy Pants
programme.desc: A panel off the world's fastest airplane, the SR-71 Blackbird, soars by the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Bang Bang
programme.desc: Join the front lines as Rick gets spooked when a WWII embalming kit invades the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Birthday Blues
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars are entertained by a military ID that has been signed by Marilyn Monroe. Another customer brings in a first edition of Beethoven's 6th Symphony.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Pawn Apocalypse
programme.desc: A pair of vintage Aerosmith tour shirts jam into the shop. Then, Rick and Corey check out a U.S. Navy foghorn. Later, Corey braces himself for the apocalypse when he checks out a state-of-the-art survival vehicle, built to fight zombies.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Up, Up and Away
programme.desc: An original Superman costume catches Rick and Corey's attention. Rick wants to fetch a good price for a retro computer, while Corey determines the value of two vintage music posters. Chumlee tries to get a good deal for a 1980s Rhodes piano.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Pawning Reagan
programme.desc: A rare double lock musket finds its way to the shop. An autographed photo of Laurel and Hardy is offered to Rick. A foam Ronald Reagan head has Corey intrigued.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Can't Pawn Me Love
programme.desc: The Venetian Hotel hosts some pricey illustrations from the children's book, 'Where the Wild Things Are,' which greatly intrigue Rick. At the shop, an ultra-rare Beatles vinyl collection impresses Chum. Chum also finds an antique hot dog bun warmer.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Fully Vested
programme.desc: A pretty pair of 18th century breechloading pistols mosey into the shop. A seller pops by with a paperweight by renowned glass artist, Paul Stankard. A custom, harmonica-holding vest that was made for Blues Traveler's front man, John Popper, stuns Chum.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Pawn Off the Grid
programme.desc: Rick explores a Funko Pop! Mystery Box, while Corey and Chumlee enlist Rick Dale to rescue their fire alarm system. Then, the Pawn Stars get on their motorcycles to ride to Oregon to spruce up Rick's ranch with some new equipment.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Sign of the Times
programme.desc: Rick Dale joins Rick to prove to Corey that he struck oil with an old Gulf sign. Then, a 1930s collectible from the Indy 500 sends Chumlee racing, and Corey snoops out what might be a Charles Schulz original illustration of Charlie Brown, and more.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Million Dollar Deal
programme.desc: A signed letter from a former president, a giant bird egg and a violin clock enter the shop. Plus, Rick heads to London to check out a work of art with a million-dollar asking price.

programme.title: American Pickers: Deuce Digging
programme.desc: The guys hit the alleys of small town Illinois where Mike bids on the rusty frame of the most famous hotrod of all time. The guys get unprecedented access to a family's dusty collection.

programme.title: American Pickers: Risks and Rewards
programme.desc: In Detroit, Frank takes a big risk on one of the greatest pieces he's ever seen. Plus, Mike acquires a Purple Heart along with a very special mission.

programme.desc: Astonishing new evidence and modern-day UFO sightings have experts rethinking ancient otherworldly Native American myths.

programme.desc: Startling evidence suggests contact with interstellar travelers in the distant past may have influenced mankind and many of our sacred sites.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: Area 52
programme.desc: Few have ever heard about the ultra top-secret military base in the remote deserts of Utah known as Area 52.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: Nazi UFO Secrets
programme.desc: Shocking evidence reveals the development of secret Nazi weapons not long after an alleged UFO crashed in Germany's Black Forest. UFO experts investigate whether the US government co-opted this technology for our own space and military programs.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: Ancient UFO Earth Landings

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence: UFO Abduction

programme.title: American Monster: In Sickness and in Health

programme.desc: Diana Lovejoy and husband Greg Mulvihill live happily in southern California. But when accusations rock the marriage and a mysterious phone call ends with an attempted murder, police uncover the shocking truth behind this once dream couple.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn: A Test of Patience
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of a teenager torments a small midwestern city for decades until a relentless team of investigators finally finds the clue that breaks the case wide open.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn: Deadly Deception
programme.desc: A father of four is gunned down during a vicious attack, but as police investigate the murder, they discover that nothing about the crime is what it appears to be.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: The Cop and the Killer
programme.desc: When Walt Chavis joined the police force, he was prepared to risk his life, but he never imagined the greatest threat he would face would be his own brother or that one day he would be forced to choose between his duty and his family.

programme.title: American Monster: Looks Can Kill
programme.desc: When mysterious and beautiful Rebecca Thudin meets the dashing Larry Fenton, a whirlwind romance is sparked. The rich, successful pair seem to be a match made in heaven, until a murder exposes a tangled web of lies, deceit and buried secrets.

programme.title: American Monster: Sing for the Camera
programme.desc: In the summer of 2014, a peaceful community in Hartford, WI is shaken to its core when a local teenager is found dead, the victim of a brutal murder, and the second victim in two days. The community must recognize that a killer lives among them.

programme.title: In Pursuit with John Walsh: When Good Meets Evil
programme.desc: John profiles Juan Lazaro Abrego, a man believed to have killed his ex-girlfriend, while Callahan investigates Derek Dean, a man accused of shooting a pair of identical twins, killing one and wounding the other.

programme.title: In Pursuit with John Walsh: Murder in Appalachia
programme.desc: John Walsh investigates how Eric Jones, the son of a preacher, allegedly turned to murder. Later, Callahan Walsh tries to make sense of how a dispute over a dog could provoke Ricardo Villanueva Cordova to reportedly commit murder in broad daylight.

programme.title: In Pursuit with John Walsh: Deadly Double Life
programme.desc: John Walsh investigates the double life of Mike Bullinger. Callahan Walsh investigates the case of Tomas Magallon Gonzalez.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Hell Hounds
programme.desc: A woman in Dayton, Ohio welcomes a young family when they move into the long derelict house next door. But escalating fights over everything from treatment of pets to treatment of children leads to a violent, bloody, and deadly confrontation.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Next Door to Crazy
programme.desc: A teacher and her son get a new start in a Las Vegas home, but soon find themselves the targets of vicious harassment and escalating intimidation by the woman who lives next door. Her fury knows no limits, and there's no telling how far she will go.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Kill Thy Neighbor
programme.desc: In Lauderdale Lakes, Florida, a single man moves into his dream home beside a single woman who is looking to start a romantic relationship with him. When he rejects her, bitter animosity ensues, followed by mutual sabotage and, eventually, gunfire.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Nail in the Coffin
programme.desc: The promise of peaceful life in Tamarac, Florida is broken when the son of a newly arrived family befriends the neighborhood's most unruly kids. Bad blood between the neighbors boils over until things come to a horrifically violent end on Halloween.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Hanging by a Thread
programme.desc: Tanya Buschman is just getting out of a nasty marriage when she falls for handsome Michel Bérubé, a bartender in Montreal. But it's not long before jealousy becomes foreplay for a suspicious suicide that has friends pointing fingers at friends.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Unholy Trinity
programme.desc: Amanda and Tonch Weldon seem like the quintessential Iowa farming couple. But behind closed doors, their cravings are quite kinky. Their secret swinging lifestyle works well for years… Until one day a deadly love triangle emerges.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Crazy Love
programme.desc: Pamela and Michael fall back in love, twenty years after the high school sweethearts break up. But the issues that first drove them apart have intensified over the years—and their second chance at love turns deadly.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Nurses Lara Crockett and Ricardo Muscolino meet on the night shift. He's ambitious and sets about creating a rich life for them and she's happy to go along.

programme.title: America: Facts vs. Fiction: Bonus Round: Discovering America
programme.desc: Explore the truth behind the men who discovered the New World and take a trip to discover the real facts behind the most famous bridge and road in America.

programme.title: America: Facts vs. Fiction: Bonus Round: Striking it Rich
programme.desc: Discover the real facts behind America's biggest economic boom and biggest bust, and learn the truth behind some of Las Vegas' greatest myths.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Charles de Gaulle
programme.desc: June 16, 1940. Paris has fallen to the Nazis and the German conquest of France is almost complete. Prime Minister Henri Petain plans to sign an armistice with Nazi Germany, but French General Charles de Gaulle secedes and rebels against the policy.

programme.title: World War II in Color: The Soviet Steamroller
programme.desc: By mid-1943, Hitler was on the run. The reality of a war on two fronts had caught up with him. In the West an Allied assault on Italy threatened his southern flank. In the East, exhausted German troops were fighting against the Soviet military machine.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Battle for Sugar Loaf
programme.desc: Taking an otherwise nondescript hill on the embattled island of Okinawa proved to be one of the most incredible battles of WWII. The young weary marines of Charlie Company strained against the searing Pacific sun to see the top of Sugar Loaf Hill.

programme.title: Air Aces: Red Tails
programme.desc: Pressure from African-American activist groups pushed President Roosevelt to initiate a Blacks-only fighter pilot training program during WWII.

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War: Eastern Front
programme.desc: The war reaches a critical turning point as Hitler expands the war to a second front. German soldiers endure the unrelenting climate of the Eastern Front, and face the full-force of angry Red Army fighters.

programme.title: Against the Odds: The Battle for Sugar Loaf

programme.title: America: Facts vs. Fiction: Bonus Round: Striking it Rich

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: Whitey Bulger: Boston Mob King
programme.desc: In 1995, ruthless murderer and Boston crime boss James 'Whitey' Bulger flees Boston to escape an impending arrest. State police, the FBI and US Marshals all join an international hunt for this violent fugitive.

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: American Gangster
programme.desc: Frank Lucas was one of the most successful heroin dealers of the 1970's. His brand of heroin known as 'Blue Magic' was notorious on the streets of New York and New Jersey.

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: Colombian Rambo
programme.desc: In 1980 the trickle of cocaine coming in to Florida suddenly becomes a flood and drug wars erupt on the streets. Carlos Lehder is behind it all. The DEA must take him down before he destroys the USA with the drug he calls Latin America's 'atomic bomb'.

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: El Chapo: Cartel Killer
programme.desc: Joaquín Guzmán Loera AKA El Chapo is America's number one illegal drug supplier. After over a decade of searching, the DEA, Homeland Security and the U.S. marshals combine forces with the Mexican marines to take him down.

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: The Black Widow
programme.desc: A cocaine empire triggers a tidal wave of deaths across the United States. Behind this rampage is a bloodthirsty female psychopath who kills for fun. Known as The Black Widow, this is the true story of Griselda Blanco and how she's brought to justice.

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: Merchant of Death
programme.desc: The DEA are running an undercover mission codenamed 'Operation Relentless.' This is a multi-million dollar, international sting with one aim, to take down Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout also known as the Merchant of Death.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Butch Cassidy: The Perfect Criminal
programme.desc: Until the day he died, whenever that was, nobody ever outsmarted Butch Cassidy. He always got away. Cassidy was an outlaw who lived more by his wits than by his gun.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Tom Horn: Grim Reaper of the Rockies
programme.desc: As the Wild West becomes settled, this mysterious hit man kills for men who want to preserve the old order where violence rules all. But the law isn't giving in this time.

programme.title: Gunslingers: John Wesley Hardin: The Dark Heart of Texas
programme.desc: In the turbulent aftermath of the Civil War, a volatile gunman wages a bloody crusade against anyone who stands between himself and his notions of freedom.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Wild Bill: Marksman and Marked Man
programme.desc: When the most famous man in the Old West outlives his legend, he becomes the target of gunslingers eager to make legends of their own by shooting him dead.

programme.title: Gunslingers: The Tombstone Vendetta
programme.desc: When one of the frontier's bravest men discovers the limits of the law in the most dangerous boomtown in the West, he exacts his own brand of justice on the cowboys who shot his brothers.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: White Mountain
programme.desc: With help from a team of friends, an avid outdoorsman takes on the challenge of building a cabin in the remote White Mountains region of Alaska. The crew must work through snow, wind and mud to haul materials and build the cabin in just seven days.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Colorado Crest
programme.desc: Builder Phil Parr heads to the wilderness of northern Colorado to build the ultimate off-grid tent. Far beyond simple nylon and poles, this lavish tent has a custom fabricated wooden frame, canvas shell and 650-square-feet of livable elegance.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Colorado Mountain Lodges
programme.desc: A Colorado couple attempts to build one of the most energy-efficient homesteads in the state. Mother Nature and bleeding edge technologies give them a run for their money as they race to complete their high-end alpine lodge before winter hits.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Blacksmith Breakroom
programme.desc: The crew build a pioneer blacksmith shop made from reclaimed beams, barnwood and rafters to serve as their office breakroom. Mark helps set up the frame, then a local blacksmith installs the forge and gives the guys an anvil lesson.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Antique barns and cabins are salvaged and the wood reused for stunning modern homes.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Bank Barn Brawl
programme.desc: The builders head back to Brookville, Pennsylvania, to save the frame of a bank barn that sits dangerously close to a road. Then, Mark visits an old log lodge resort and scouts an amazing timber frame barn built in 1895.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders: Saving More than Logs in Bulls Gap, TN
programme.desc: Mark and the guys go the extra mile to save the antique logs from a Tennessee home in distress. During the job they meet Larry, who grew up in the log cabin with no running water.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Big Family, Bigger Beach Home
programme.desc: A couple's Dewey Beach home has become cramped and in need of reconfiguration. With a new open-concept layout, Marnie aims to preserve the integrity of the house while improving the flow and maximizing space.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Captain's Paradise
programme.desc: A former ship captain and his wife decide to buy a classic beach cottage a block away from the water. The location is perfect but the interior of the home has structural and layout flaws.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Amazing A-Frame
programme.desc: A couple turns to Marnie to help them transform their shabby A-frame retreat into a beautiful family beach home.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Open-Concept Canal Home
programme.desc: Karen's 30-year-old canal-side home has an enormous backyard and attractive deck area, but its choppy 1980s layout and awkward additions aren't functional for entertaining. Marnie has big plans for it including an indoor and outdoor kitchen renovation.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Beauty and the Beach
programme.desc: A townhouse overlooking Bethany Beach is dated and awkward. Marnie attempts to update the home, but the discovery of water damage may affect her ability to make the most of its dazzling view.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Save It or Sell It
programme.desc: A couple loves Lewes and the cheerful home they own there, but their two daughters have outgrown the space. Now, the family's torn between selling the house or renovating it. Marnie tries to expand the home without losing its historic charm.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: From Soaked to Saved
programme.desc: A couple has invested in a 50-year-old abandoned home in Dewey Beach, Delaware. They decide to hand the reins over to Marnie in the hopes she'll turn the fixer-upper into a safe vacation destination for their two boys.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: The Beach Gut
programme.desc: A couple transports a 60-year-old house to a plot of land near the beach in the hopes it can be transformed into the perfect beach getaway. Marnie must renovate the home while keeping its rustic charm from the 1960s.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Flood-Ridden to Fabulous
programme.desc: Due to the layout of their property, Tom and Kim's spacious backyard gets overrun by flooding during thunderstorms. They enlist Marnie to make the yard flood-proof and transform the impractical space into an outdoor living area.

programme.title: Big Beach Builds: Backyard Beauty
programme.desc: Being close to the water in South Bethany, Delaware means unwanted wear and tear on Jay and Christine's back deck. They enlist Marnie to update and renovate the space.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Off-The-Grid Renovation
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team renovate an off-the-grid hunting cabin deep in the woods of Oxford, ME. They have a budget of $20,000 and a time frame of six weeks. They face many challenges associated with building off-the-grid.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Kids' Dream Lake House
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team help a DIY homeowner jumpstart his project and renovate with kids in mind. With a budget of $30,000 and a July 1st deadline, the masters are going to have to use their imagination on this one.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Rebuilding The Old Schoolhouse
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team of Cabin Masters hike through the woods of Dedham, ME, to restore a dilapidated family camp that was once the old schoolhouse. A serious case of rot threatens to close down the entire project.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: The Morrill Family Bunkhouse
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team of builders take on the old army barracks on their family camp that has served as overflow sleeping for over 40 years.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: It Takes a Village
programme.desc: Founded in 1935, Agassiz Village has provided underprivileged and at-risk kids an opportunity to experience camp. Chase and his team take on a big project with a small budget to help the camp with the support of the community.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Ocean City, Maryland
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Ocean City, Maryland, to dive into a massive jumbo lump crab cake sandwich, make some of the area's iconic salt water taffy candies and fight to stay afloat in the Titanic Sundae Challenge.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Providence, Rhode Island
programme.desc: Adam visits Providence, Rhode Island for a unique grilled pizza and for the infamous Triple Murder Burger. Adam's journey concludes with him coaching a rugby player through 15 fully loaded hot wieners in 45 minutes.

programme.title: Dinner at Tiffani's: Tiffani's French Cafe
programme.desc: Tiffani transports her friends to a French cafe with a menu inspired by her time spent in Paris. On the menu, a croque monsieur-inspired grilled cheese, with crispy Frites and Homemade Aioli.

programme.title: Dinner at Tiffani's: Breakfast For Dinner
programme.desc: Tiffani hosts a PJ party for her friends Bree Turner and Marla Sokoloff, serving up her favorite morning dishes at night. Recipes include, savory potato pancakes and sausage and goat cheese egg casserole.

programme.desc: Bobby takes his favorite green market finds to make a healthy weekend brunch. His menu features tomato strata, zucchini pancakes with apricot butter, and seared salmon with a crunchy Brussels sprouts-apple salad.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: French-American Brunch
programme.desc: Chef Bobby Flay adds a hint of American influence to French Bistro style dishes.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Decadent Regional Dishes

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Juicy, Crunchy and Tangy
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in Lexington, Ky. for a plump pork burger and ingenious smoked chicken wings smothered in a blackberry jalapeno sauce.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Eggs Men
programme.desc: Noah Cappe gets the royal treatment with "ye olde eats" at the Hoggetown Medieval Faire in Gainesville, Fla. Meat is all over the menu, and Noah goes wild for the smoked bison burger and the wild boar leg.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Dates on a Plane
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives in the California desert for a date with deliciousness at the Riverside County Fair and National Date Festival. He tries spicy treats like the fire fingers kartwheel and the sonoran fire dog.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Mad Snacks: Berry Road
programme.desc: Noah Cappe travels back in time at the Texas Renaissance Festival, where the medieval-inspired meals range from the Sir Francis fry dog to the crown jewel. Then it's over to Plant City, Fla., where Noah chows down on some berry delicious carnival treats.

programme.desc: The Iowa State Fair welcomes Noah Cappe with a trio of meaty masterpieces, but it's the double Dutch almond funnel that has everyone jumping with joy.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Shepherd's Pie and Sunday Dinner Sundaes

programme.desc: Bodhi faces his fear to earn his final stamp towards becoming a Super Flier. After Mia gets a thorn stuck in her paw, she worries it will hurt when K.C. tries to take it out.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Monkeying Around And Around/ Koala Kuisine
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy have to deliver a monkey; K.C. cooks something for a baby hippo.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Keia's New Doghouse / The Fang Fairy
programme.desc: After Bob builds a doghouse for Keia, the pugs encourage her to host a puppy housewarming party. Rolly loses his first puppy tooth and can't wait for a visit from the Fang Fairy!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Captain Rolly/ The Coolest Dogs in Town
programme.desc: Bob takes Bingo and Rolly to a 'Captain Dog!' event where the pugs get to be heroes for a day. The pups try to find ways to keep Bob cool on a hot summer day.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: A Light for the Lighthouse / Music City Mishap
programme.desc: When the pugs find out that the light on Bob's favourite lighthouse isn't working, they go on a mission to fix it. Then, While in Nashville, Bingo and Rolly find a lost guitar and go on a mission to return it to its rightful owner.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Fantastic Pet Force / Sea No Turtle

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Puppy Problems/ The Mighty Little Dragon
programme.desc: A tenacious penguin and a kind hearted flamingo train to be delivery birds.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: For Lion Out Loud/ Porcupine Panic
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy teach a lion cub the difference between his "indoor" and "outdoor" roar. When a porcupine is scared of the delivery tube, K.C. and the Fliers help him beat his fear.

programme.title: Doc McStuffins: Get Set to Get Wet/ Loud Louie
programme.desc: Doc teaches Melinda Mermaid how to swim. When the volume control on Doc's toy cell phone Louis gets stuck, she diagnoses him with 'Superlouditis' and teaches him how to use his inside voice.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The Lab Four / Feelin' Antsy
programme.desc: When a performance by Bob's favourite band, The Walruses, is canceled, Bingo and Rolly make it their mission to ensure the show goes on. Then, when ants invade playcare, the pugs must find a way to get them out.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Don't Rain on My Pug-rade/ Their Royal Pug-ness
programme.desc: When Bob can't leave his house to watch a parade, the pups enlist a family of ducks to march past the house. Bingo and Rolly head to England to learn how a queen is treated.

programme.desc: A woman and her daughter take in a lost dog and give him a home. The dog turns out to belong to a baseball player, who works out a shared custody since the girl has fallen in love with the dog.

programme.title: Movie: Love in the Sun
programme.desc: A recently engaged woman returns to her hometown where she finds herself falling for another man. She now must decide if she wants to be with her current fiancée, or, if she wants to explore life with her new beau.

programme.title: The Office: Phyllis' Wedding
programme.desc: Phyllis regrets giving Michael an important job at her wedding after several embarrassing moments. Pam has flashbacks of her own wedding planning when she sees Phyllis's decorations. Meanwhile, Dwight is determined to catch wedding crashers.

programme.title: The Office: Business School
programme.desc: Ryan invites Michael to be a guest speaker at his business school. Meanwhile, Dwight battles a bat that gets loose in the office while Pam invites co-workers to her first art show.

programme.title: The Office: Cocktails
programme.desc: Michael and Jan go public with their relationship during a party at their CFO's home. At the party, Jim meets Karen's ex. Meanwhile, the rest of the office decides to go to happy hour for drinks.

programme.title: The Office: The Negotiation
programme.desc: Darryl decides to meet with Michael to negotiate a pay increase. Meanwhile, Jim deals with the consequences of Pam's confession to Roy.

programme.title: The Office: Safety Training
programme.desc: Andy returns to the office after several weeks of anger management training, determined to make a fresh start with all the Dunder Mifflin employees. On safety training day, Michael and Dwight try to illuminate the true dangers of the workplace.

programme.title: The Office: Product Recall
programme.desc: The office is in crisis mode after a large shipment of Dunder-Mifflin paper is accidentally sent out with an obscene watermark. In an effort to manage the media, Michael holds an impromptu press conference.

programme.title: The Office: Women's Appreciation
programme.desc: After Phyllis gets flashed in the parking lot, Dwight sets out to improve on-site security. Michael uses the situation as a pretence to bond with the female employees at the mall and to get advice on how to break up with Jan.

programme.title: The Office: Beach Games
programme.desc: Michael is being considered for a job at corporate and has to recommend his replacement.

programme.title: The Office: The Job
programme.desc: The office is still buzzing from the events of their day at the beach. A job opening in Corporate pits coworker against coworker as Michael, Jim and Karen all head to New York for final interviews.

programme.title: The Closer: Cherry Bomb
programme.desc: A 16-year-old girl is found dead and questions lead to the son of an L.A. County Sheriff. As Johnson investigates, she learns that the son may be hiding behind his father's badge and may have done so before.

programme.title: The Closer: Live Wire
programme.desc: When a reporter alerts Brenda to a crime scene, Priority Homicide and the FBI fight for jurisdiction over the case. Meanwhile, interpersonal relationships on the squad force Brenda into a corner.

programme.title: The Closer: Dial 'M' for Provenza
programme.desc: Things spiral out of control when Provenza goes undercover to look into a murder for hire.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: See One, Do One, Teach One
programme.desc: Detective Rizzoli and medical examiner Isles investigate the grisly murder of a wealthy couple. The M.O. matches the work of a serial killer known as the Surgeon. Now that the Surgeon is behind bars, it seems a protégé is picking up where he left off.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Boston Strangler Redux
programme.desc: Tensions are high as the squad investigates a possible copycat of the legendary Boston Strangler.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Sympathy for the Devil
programme.desc: Jane and Maura investigate the mysterious death of a young boy who may have died during an attempted exorcism.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: She Works Hard for the Money
programme.desc: After a student is gunned down in broad daylight, the investigation reveals she had more than a few secrets. Meanwhile, Jane's mother Angela gets into a spat with her father over the car.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The eldest brother in a Boston Brahmin family is found in the harbour dead after an apparent sailing accident. Maura's close connection with the family and Jane's resentment over their wealth threaten to derail the investigation.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Jane and Maura investigate what appears to be a hate crime: the brutal assault and murder of a woman leaving a lesbian bar. With Jane going undercover, they hope to lure the killer out in the open.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Road Rage
programme.desc: Bloody sneakers and a purse found in a sleeping bag leads police to the body of a young woman. After collecting, sorting, and analyzing all sorts of evidence, it's a simple mark on the woman's body that will solve the case.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Hunter or Hunted?
programme.desc: A women is killed in the woods on the last day of hunting season, and police assume it was an accident until a suspicious letter arrives.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Without a Prayer
programme.desc: Madalyn Murray O'Hair and her two top lieutenants disappeared with no shortage of suspects. Her abduction may never have been solved if it weren't for the discovery of a headless, handless body, and the serial number on an artificial hip.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Clutch of Witnesses
programme.desc: Two eyewitnesses see a truck intentionally drive over a sleeping man outside a motorcycle gang's clubhouse. During the investigation the witnesses, the truck, and the body disappear, leaving police just one clue: a bloodstain in the snow.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Scout's Honor
programme.desc: Police determine that the human remains found in a discarded box belong to one Edna Posey. But to find out whether the man accused of the crime is guilty, investigators turn to insects and the forensic entomologist who can interpret their behavior.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Touching Recollection
programme.desc: An eighteen-year-old girl is abducted at gunpoint from her home in Jackson, Ohio. Fortunately the victim returns hours later, and police hope the tire impressions and shoe prints at the crime scene will enable them to find the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Big Chill
programme.desc: When a woman dies after a long illness, investigators have to determine if her death was due to natural causes, suicide, or murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bound for Jail
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead in a ravine, police are stuck, as any evidence was washed away by storms. 20 years after the fact, an old hat and a tiny chip of metal put investigators back on the trail of a case long gone cold.

programme.title: Very Scary People: Robert Durst: World's Worst Fugitive (Part 1)
programme.desc: The unbelievable story of Robert Durst, the millionaire heir to a vast real estate fortune who has been linked to the deaths of his wife, his best friend, and his neighbor.

programme.title: Very Scary People: Robert Durst: Nobody Tells the Whole Truth (Part 2)
programme.desc: Six weeks after jumping bail in Galveston, Texas, Robert Durst is caught on a store security camera in Pennsylvania shoplifting a sandwich and is quickly sent back to Galveston to stand trial for the murder of his neighbor, Morris Black.

programme.title: Forensic Files: For Love or Money
programme.desc: A wealthy man goes missing, and leaves behind instructions in his will regarding what should be done if he died of violent circumstances, leading to believe he was aware his life was in danger.

programme.title: Forensic Files: South of the Border
programme.desc: The murder of an American tourist in Mexico seems to be burglary related. However, closer examination by law officials both sides of the border reveal a far more sinister truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Palm Print Conviction
programme.desc: A woman leaves a bar with a stranger only to have her body discovered the next morning.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Vow of Silence
programme.desc: When a man's friends report his mail-order bride missing, police soon orient their investigation around the husband. While following up on some leads, they dig up some evidence tying him to deaths of two other wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Elephant Tracks
programme.desc: A jeweler notices that a victim wore an elephant pendant that had been pawned in her shop.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Bag of Evidence
programme.desc: When a 9-year old girl disappears, an anonymous caller leads police to her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Traces of Truth
programme.desc: A high school gym teacher took time off from class to give his friend a ride home -- and never returned. Investigators suspected foul play, but without a body the case would be difficult to prove.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Honor Thy Father
programme.desc: A 16-year-old girl is killed by her mother and father in her own home. The parents say they acted in self-defence but the forensic evidence indicates otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Road Rage

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Who Killed Chandra Levy?
programme.desc: Intern Chandra Levy vanishes in May of 2001 in Washington, D.C. Her cell phone and keys are found by police in her apartment that shows no signs of trouble, leading them to question where she went and who saw her last.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Jodi Arias: Dirty Little Secret
programme.desc: Four years after the vicious killing of Travis Alexander, his ex-girlfriend Jodi Arias stands trial for his murder. Millions of people are captivated by the lurid details of their relationship.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Jodi Arias: Sex, Lies, and Photographs
programme.desc: Jodi Arias is a pretty, small town waitress and aspiring photographer. Travis Alexander is a handsome, devout Mormon, and successful businessman. Months after their sexually charged romance began, Travis was found brutally murdered in his shower at home.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Paintball
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15 year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered. It would take 24 years to bring her killer to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sign Here
programme.desc: A young woman walked out of a department store, got into a rental car, and was never seen again. The signature on the rental car agreement turned out to be a forgery.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shadow of a Doubt
programme.desc: An unknown assailant walked into a retail store and fired three shots, killing the owner. Police had suspects, but all of them had alibis.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Bite marks lead the police to a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Past Lives: The Madison Rutherford Case
programme.desc: The body of a wealthy American businessman is found in his rental car. When the victim's wife files a claim for $7 million of life insurance, investigators seek the help of a forensic anthropologist and uncover a chilling scheme of fraud and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Over and Out
programme.desc: A pipe bomb explodes in a house, killing a man and wounding his mother. Police use a lot number on a battery and a mailing label to track down the bomber, and determine his motive.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Megalodon
programme.desc: Josh Gates dives into shark infested waters to investigate what drove the sixty foot long Megalodon shark to extinction.

programme.title: Tyson Vs. Jaws: Rumble on the Reef
programme.desc: Mike Tyson learns how to TKO a shark in the name of research as he goes up against sharks.

programme.title: Shark Lockdown: Sharkmania
programme.desc: With Sharkmania facts and bonus scenes: As a global lockdown quiets the ocean, New Zealand's biggest great whites come out of hiding and expert Kina Scollay is on a quest to find a monster.

programme.title: Abandoned Waters
programme.desc: Lack of human activity on the water caused by the global lockdown has led the largest great whites in South Australia to return to their natural behaviors. Shark experts can now study them up close and personal in ways that were nearly impossible before.

programme.title: ShaqAttack
programme.desc: Shaq and his friends Dude Perfect and Mark Rober investigate which shark has the perfect predatory attack.

programme.title: Jaws Awakens
programme.desc: Jeff Kurr and Dickie Chivell are joined by shark expert Chris Fallows in the search for the world's largest male great white shark, and they find an almost 20-foot long, two-ton shark in the waters of New Zealand.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: After the Hunt: Josh Gates Tonight: Totally Jaw-some
programme.desc: Josh Gates' epic Shark Week 2020 adventure continues with basketball great and 'ShaqAttack' star Shaquille O'Neal, 'Star Trek' legend William Shatner, and Shark Week daredevil Dickie Chivell. Josh also dives into the Georgia Aquarium's tiger shark tank.

programme.title: Shark Week Immersion

programme.title: Isle of Jaws: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: A team ventures to the famed Isle of Jaws off the West Australian coast to find answers to whether great whites can recognize and prefer the company of a blood relative.

programme.title: Jungle Shark
programme.desc: Marine biologist Dr. Craig O'Connell and Andy Casagrande travel up the Serena River in the rainforests of Costa Rica to find out why young bull sharks swim up the river, and how they avoid the American crocodiles living there.

programme.title: Shark Week with Baby Shark: Baby Shark and Hammerhead Shark
programme.desc: There are many different types of sharks in the ocean. Meet Baby Shark's new shark friend, Hammerhead Shark!

programme.title: Jaws of the Deep
programme.desc: Marine biologist Greg Skomal and the REMUS SharkCam team return to Guadalupe to find the world's largest great white shark, Deep Blue. Using two robot subs, they uncover hunting techniques and ambush strategies of great whites.

programme.title: Air Jaws Returns
programme.desc: In 2001, Jeff Kurr joined Chris Fallows off the shores of South Africa to witness great whites launching out of the ocean as they attack seals. 17 years and 7 shows later, Jeff and Chris travel to New Zealand in search of the next Air Jaws hotspot.

programme.title: Sharks vs. Dolphins: Face Off
programme.desc: Dr. Mike Heithaus and his team offer new research that may solve why sharks attack dolphins far more than we ever knew.

programme.title: Cuba's Secret Shark Lair
programme.desc: In 1945, one of the largest sharks ever recorded was captured in Cuban waters, a great white named 'El Monstruo.' Two teams of researchers divide and conquer in search of Cuba's massive great hammerhead in 'The Queen' and 'El Monstruo'-sized great white.

programme.title: Monster Mako: Perfect Predator

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Whose Phone Is Ringing?

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hellcopter
programme.desc: The joker that required medical attention during the fat foot race punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Dark Side of the Moon
programme.desc: The guys make passes at supermarket shoppers, then pose as reporters. Then, with a painfully invasive punishment, tonight's loser is the butt of the joke.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Statue of Limitations
programme.desc: The guys pose as inhospitable restaurant hosts, then beg strangers to babysit their grandfathers. Plus the longest punishment in Jokers' history.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Wrapper's Delight
programme.desc: The guys make a mockery of each other while acting as restaurant managers, sing along with strangers trying to name that (made up) tune. The loser gets wrapped up in a birthday surprise that definitely wasn't on his wish list.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Smushed
programme.desc: Joe, Q, Sal and Murr give a security presentation that’s anything but safe, then hold hands with strangers in the park.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: You're Cut Off
programme.desc: The guys pose as employees at a copy centre, conduct bizarre exit interviews for moviegoers and then lead some very peculiar protests. Later, it's unhappy hour for tonight's big loser, as he cuts off paying customers at a party.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Toasted
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Sal and Q get in a pickle serving up humiliating tasks as deli waiters, and later, they step behind the counter asking customers to settle a series of silly debates. Plus, tonight's big loser gets burned during an embarrassing toast.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bidder Loser
programme.desc: A passersby reaction to Sal burying his niece in the park, the rare outcome of a Joker vs. Joker challenge.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Everything's Just Rosie
programme.desc: How many pounds of potatoes were made for the famous Scoopski Potatoes challenge.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Drum and Drummer
programme.desc: So many shoppers in the candy aisle during 'Star Struck'. The celeb that was on set while the guys filmed 'Brain Games'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stuffed Turkey
programme.desc: The amount of times Joe showed his butt on the show. Why the volunteers were turned away for Tool Bag.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Marathon Man
programme.desc: Find out which joker has pet parakeets, why the crew had to work in a truck down the block while filming at a bagel shop and who got to sound the start horn for Murr's race across the Brooklyn Bridge.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Guilty as Charged
programme.desc: Find out why Q messed with a fishmonger at Fairway instead of a shopper, what nearly caused Sal to lose it during his UFO presentation and how the crew nabbed close ups from inside the mob during Q's punishment.

programme.title: The Misery Index: They Didn't Blur the Dog!

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Chick Magnet

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Hump Day
programme.desc: The guys geek out while lending a hand to customers at a casual restaurant, then battle across the table of a focus group. Plus, tonight's big loser gets spitting mad when forced to spend some quality time at the zoo.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Lords of the Ring
programme.desc: The guys skirt the law while posing as receptionists, then go fishing at an unlikely locale. And the heat is on tonight's big losers as they try to give one unlucky customer the deal of a lifetime.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Guilty as Charged

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr cruise the food court sending texts to strangers, then throw caution, and groceries, to the wind. And tonight's loser is cast as a genie in a play but wishes he could be anywhere else.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Miss Brown's Little Helpers

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Carolina Comfort

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: 'Too Cute' Brunch
programme.desc: Kardea invites her Aunt TC over for a big low-country brunch. The perfectly Southern meal includes Charleston-style shrimp and grits, homemade buttermilk biscuits topped with fresh apricot jam.

programme.title: Delicious Miss Brown: Traveling Supper Club
programme.desc: Kardea hosts an intimate traveling supper club in her hometown of Charleston, S.C. She creates a Southern-inspired menu of low country spatchcocked chicken served with traditional Gullah red rice and a warm asparagus salad.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: I Like Big Boats and I Cannot Lie
programme.desc: Captain Sandy Yawn is at the helm of the mega-yacht Wellington, cruising one of the most desired yachting locations in the Mediterranean: Mallorca, Spain. Malia returns as Captain Sandy's first female Bosun, a role she hopes to grow into.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Can't Touch This!
programme.desc: Hannah struggles to get her Second Stew in line, and tensions reach a boiling point when Lara refuses to take direction from Hannah. In the galley, Kiko finds himself up against guest that are less than satisfied. Malia deals with a green deck crew.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: The Italian's Job
programme.desc: Romance is in bloom on the crew's first night out of the season as Pete makes his move while Rob and Jess find themselves connecting on a deeper level. The second charter kicks off when guest with ties to the music industry arrive.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Ace of Stew Face
programme.desc: With bad weather rolling in and down her second stew, Hannah scrambles to manage the interior with only Jess to rely on. Captain Sandy and Malia draw a hard line with the deck team when they call the guys out for their questionable behaviour.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Bringing the Thunder
programme.desc: A blast from the past returns amidst a massive thunderstorm that threatens to keep the Wellington docked for their next charter. Rob and Jessica deepen their connection, while Pete's attitude continues to frustrate Malia.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Oh Snap!
programme.desc: Kiko faces his biggest challenge yet when demanding charter guests invite friends to join a six-course dinner. Hannah confronts Bugsy about their past, while Rob and Jess find their budding relationship threatened by a new revelation.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: No Mushroom for Error
programme.desc: An injured Jess fights to maintain her spot on the boat, while another crew member’s inappropriate behavior finally catches up to him. Meanwhile, high-maintenance charter guests challenge the interior and expose cracks in Hannah and Bugsy’s relationship.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Rise and Don't Shine
programme.desc: After struggling through the first day of charter, Kilo fights to regain his footing with a demanding set of charter guests. While Hannah leans on an overworked Bugsy to carry an injured Jess, Captain Sandy becomes concerned about interior's performance.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Viva, Loss Vegas
programme.desc: On a crew night out, Alex reveals his feelings to Bugsy while Hannah struggles to connect with the group. Meanwhile, Captain Sandy comes down on Jess for her performance in the laundry room, and Malia has an incident with the water toys.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Closing Time
programme.desc: Captain Sandy and Kiko find themselves at a crossroad after a critical dinner, while Hannah fights to maintain control under the weight of a stressful season. On a guest excursion into Valldemossa, Rob and Jess up the ante in their relationship.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Cabin Fever
programme.desc: The crew enjoys a day off at an exclusive beach club in Mallorca, but concerns arise when Hannah stays behind to nurse an illness. Romance is in the air when Malia's boyfriend Tom arrives for a visit, while Alex takes stock of his relationship with Bugsy.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Decoded: Secret Presidential Codes
programme.desc: The team investigates the secret presidential codes of Thomas Jefferson and how they may be partially responsible for the death of one of the world's greatest explorers, Meriwether Lewis.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Decoded: The White House
programme.desc: The search for the White House cornerstone has been on for over 200 years, and everyone from Harry Truman to Barbara Bush have tried to find it.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: The Ground Zero Flag
programme.desc: A few hours after the iconic Ground Zero Flag was raised by firefighters on 9/11 this sacred relic simply disappeared.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: Missing Moon Rocks
programme.desc: American astronauts brought back lunar rocks from their mission to the moon. In a daring heist the rocks were stolen from NASA headquarters and are still missing.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: Lee Harvey Oswald's Ring
programme.desc: Host Brad Meltzer and viewers are on the hunt for lost and stolen history, including an historic plaque commemorating Alex Haley's Roots. He also looks for an original copy of the Bill of Rights, and a mysterious ring worn by Lee Harvey Oswald.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: Geronimo's Skull
programme.desc: Brad Meltzer looks for Geronimo's skull, rumored to be robbed from the grave by a top secret society. He also aims to recover Teddy Roosevelt's pistol, which was stolen from a museum.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: Moon Landing Tapes
programme.desc: Brad Meltzer looks for the historic Moon Tapes that hold the clearest footage from the Apollo 11 moon landing. Then, President Lincoln's brilliantly written Bixby Letter that inspired the movie Saving Private Ryan is missing.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: D-Day Footage
programme.desc: Brad enlists America's help to find lost and stolen historical artifacts. Among the missing: rare and historic footage from D-Day, shot behind enemy lines during an remarkable mission.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: JFK's Brain
programme.desc: Host Brad Meltzer and viewers are on the hunt for lost and stolen history, including JFK's actual brain tissue and millions of dollars of gold coins stolen from the U. S. Mint.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History: White House Cornerstone
programme.desc: Among the missing: the White House cornerstone, the very first piece of the White House, laid by the secretive Freemasons. Then, letters from an American soldier in Vietnam--pilfered for enemy propaganda.

programme.title: Movie: Romeo and Juliet
programme.desc: Two teenagers from feuding families fall in love at first sight and try to be together.

programme.title: Movie: Her Cardboard Lover
programme.desc: A woman hires a handsome male secretary in an effort to make her indecisive beau jealous.

programme.desc: A woman's marital problems fuel the gossip within a circle of Park Avenue women.

programme.desc: A woman falls for the son of her aunt's killer, despite her uncle's objections.

programme.desc: A romantic account of the love affair between poets Elizabeth Barett and Robert Browning.
